what_it_does: This file is a generated surface specification for view A12, declaring what UI addresses and components it builds to, its platform scope, status, and design-system build rules.
observations: It is a JSON surface-spec under a journey-spine directory tree.
The view id is A12, area A, title 'Workshop — full-detail node surface', desktop-only.
home_journey is '_unrouted' while journeys and cross_journeys are empty.
addresses list three UI URIs: ui://inspector/workshop, ui://toolbar/workshop, and ui://workshop.
build_to maps to components inspector, toolbar, and workshop; a design-lint command; and a token-to-CSS pipeline.
It explicitly states it was built by a Claude Code session and that addresses must not be invented.
standouts: The file is in the '_unrouted' journey spine despite being quality-passed.
It declares three distinct UI addresses all scoped to 'workshop'.
It embeds build tooling and token-source rules directly in the surface spec.
conventions: Follows a structured surface-spec schema with id, area, title, platforms, represents, status, journeys, addresses, and build_to.
Uses URI-style addresses (ui://...) for view references.
concerns: journeys and cross_journeys are empty while the comment says they carry the web (B3), which leaves the routing graph incomplete in this file.
The file is quality-passed but lives under '_unrouted', which may be intentional or may signal a routing gap.
notes: A desktop workshop surface spec that pins three UI addresses and build targets but has not yet been wired into any routed journeys.
questions: Are journeys/cross_journeys intentionally empty because routing is handled elsewhere, or is this file awaiting routing assignment?
Does '_unrouted' status coexist with 'quality-passed' by design?
Is the design_lint command expected to be run manually or as part of CI?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to be a generated surface-spec carrying address-refs that carry the web; the actual content is a complete component/address/build declaration with empty journey references, so the routing aspect is not yet realized.
apparent_themes: Journey-spine routing model for UI surfaces
Build-time coupling between design tokens, linting, and component targets
intention_signals: It appears to be trying to define a canonical, build-runnable surface for the workshop desktop view while remaining neutral about which journeys it belongs to.
novelty: It embeds both the UI address map and the build/lint/token pipeline in a single surface spec, treating the surface as a build target rather than only a design artifact.
contribution: It contributes a concrete, quality-passed surface definition for the workshop desktop view with explicit UI addresses, component targets, and build rules that a fused system could keep as a buildable reference.
summary_for_embedding: A generated JSON surface spec for desktop workshop view A12, listing three ui:// addresses, components inspector/toolbar/workshop, design-lint command, and token-to-CSS build rule, with empty journeys and home_journey '_unrouted'.
intention_for_embedding: This file was meant to define a build-ready, full-detail node surface for the workshop view and anchor it to specific UI addresses and build tooling.
